GT LEARN #871 ⛽ Minimum Number of Refueling Stops HARD Greedy Max-Heap
target= startFuel= stations= exp: 2
📝 Problem Statement
A car starts at position 0 with startFuel liters and wants to reach target. Each station [loc, cap] on the way can be visited; if visited, the entire cap liters get added to the tank. Each unit distance consumes 1 liter. Return the minimum number of refueling stops required, or -1 if impossible.
🧠 Solving Approach
Greedy + Max-Heap: drive as far as possible, and only when the tank goes negative, look back at all the stations we already passed and refuel from the biggest one available (top of max-heap). Repeat until tank is non-negative or heap empties (return -1). Append [target, 0] as a sentinel so the same loop body handles "reach the target" check.
🎯 Core Concepts
Max-heap of capacities: Python heapq is min-heap, so push -capacity. The top is always the largest tank-fill available among passed stations.
Greedy choice: always refuel from biggest pending station — this minimizes # of stops because more fuel per stop = fewer stops needed.
Sentinel target: appending [target, 0] turns "did we reach target?" into the same gas-deduction step.
⏱ Time: O(n log n) 💾 Space: O(n)
🧪 Test Cases (click to load)
CLASSIC2 stops needed→ 2
target=100fuel=104 stations
NO-STOPSalready enough→ 0
target=1fuel=1stations=[]
IMPOSSIBLEcant reach first→ -1
target=100fuel=1[[10,100]]
ONE-BY-ONEsmall refills→ 3
target=100fuel=50[[25,25],[50,25],[75,25]]
LARGE10 stations→ 4
target=1000fuel=8310 stations spread out
Result: —
📜 Python Code
📊 Visualization — Road, Car, Stations & Max-Heap
0/0
←→R 100%
